rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into implementation scopes only when binding implementation-level outputs or subcomponent relationships.
Preserve AADL component type and implementation boundaries by not injecting implementation-specific logic into type scopes.
generic_rules: Contract annotations must respect AADL scope: type-level for visible interfaces, implementation-level for internal bindings.
Only reference features explicitly exposed in the target scope; avoid subcomponent paths in higher-level annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax and must contain valid declarations: assume, guarantee, eq, const, or assign.
Boolean expressions for range checks should be declared as equations (eq) before being referenced in guarantees.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as variables in AGREE expressions unless explicitly mapped to ports or constants.
Do not use subcomponent port names in component type annexes; they are not visible at that scope.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
